fire_rating.ul_design: UL D902 (or equivalent restrained beam/floor assembly)

components.name: W-Beam (Wide Flange) — Supporting Beam
components.description: Wide flange beam supporting fluted metal deck above. Top flange is partially shielded by deck bearing.

components.name: Fluted Metal Deck
components.material: Galvanized steel deck — ASTM A653, Grade 33
components.description: Corrugated/fluted metal deck spanning between beams. Deck profile creates flutes (ribs) that bear on beam top flange.

properties.attachment: Puddle weld or TEK screw to beam top flange

components.name: SFRM Spray Coat — 3-Sided Application
components.description: Spray-applied fireproofing on three exposed sides of the beam: bottom of top flange (between deck flutes), both sides of web, and entire bottom flange. Top of top flange is shielded by deck.

properties.coverage_note: Must fill gap between deck flutes and beam top flange

components.name: Edge Coverage at Deck-Beam Interface
components.description: SFRM must wrap around the deck-to-beam junction to ensure fire-rated continuity. Coverage extends onto deck underside at beam edge per UL listing.

properties.extension_inches: 2-4 onto deck underside from beam flange edge
properties.critical_note: No gaps allowed between beam SFRM and deck underside SFRM

assembly_constraints.description: Minimum SFRM thickness for 3-sided condition per UL listing. 3-sided designs typically require less thickness than 4-sided due to deck shielding top flange.
assembly_constraints.rule: Use 3-sided W/D column in UL design listing thickness table

assembly_constraints.description: SFRM must fill the gap between the deck flute valleys and the beam top flange
assembly_constraints.rule: No voids permitted at deck-to-beam interface within the rated assembly zone

assembly_constraints.description: Deck must be fully attached (welded or screwed) to beam before SFRM application
assembly_constraints.rule: All deck-to-beam connections complete prior to fireproofing

assembly_constraints.description: SFRM on beam must transition continuously to deck underside coating where required by UL listing
assembly_constraints.rule: Min 2-inch overlap onto deck underside from beam flange tip
